Venus. These too must have favourable positions in the
horoscope particularly in odd signs. Similarly, in the case of
a female, Mars governs the nature of blood and the Moon
controls the strength to bear children. These two planets
should preferably occupy even signs. The following simple
rule can be easily tested in the majority of cases, unless
other elusive combinations arc present in the horoscope.
In the case of a male, add together the longitude of the
Sun, Venus and Jupiter. Expunge multiples of 360°. The
sign arrived at indicates what is called 'Beejasputa' or the
longitude of the seed. Similarly in a female horoscope, add
together the longitudes of Mars, the Moon and Jupiter.
Expunge multiples of 360°. The sign arrived at represents
the longitude of the bed (Kshetra sphuta). If Beeja sphuta
occupies an odd sign and odd Navamsa and is aspected by
or associated with benefics, then there is strength in the
seed and the person is capable of procrea-
